title: Isfahan Nuclear Complex Buildings Covered with New Roofing
detail: Satellite imagery reveals that destroyed buildings at the Isfahan nuclear complex have been covered with roofing structures, blocking overhead observation of reconstruction activities. Two tunnel entrances to underground facilities have been packed with earth as a protective measure against future strikes. Analysts from the Institute for Science and International Security assess the coverings are designed to 'obscure activity rather than repair structures for use,' suggesting Iran may be relocating or securing nuclear materials underground.

sources.name: ISIS (Institute for Science and International Security)

media.caption: Satellite imagery shows new roofing structures over damaged Isfahan nuclear complex buildings

title: 57% of Iranians Experiencing Some Level of Malnourishment
detail: Iran's Ministry of Social Welfare data from late 2024, now widely cited by international media, shows that 57% of Iranians are experiencing some level of malnourishment. The figure, which predates the December fuel price hikes and the latest phase of currency collapse, is expected to worsen significantly. Working-class families in Tehran and provincial cities report spending over 70% of household income on food alone, with meat and dairy already unaffordable for much of the population.

media.caption: Data reveals 57% of Iranians experiencing some level of malnourishment amid economic crisis

title: Customs Data Shows Sharp Decline in Essential Imports
detail: Iranian customs data reveals a significant drop in imports of animal feed, fertilizers, and agricultural inputs since the elimination of the preferential exchange rate on December 2. The loss of the subsidized forex window has made importing basic agricultural commodities three times more expensive overnight. Livestock farmers warn of an impending meat shortage as feed costs become prohibitive. Pharmaceutical importers report similar difficulties, with letters of credit increasingly difficult to secure from sanctioned banks.

media.caption: Iran's essential imports decline sharply after elimination of subsidized exchange rate
